one_line_headline: At a K=200-target validation budget, the trust layer's default-deny abstention on the uncalibrated (cross-dataset) regime — where the naive predictor's precision collapses to ~0 vs a 0.20 random base rate — avoids ~160–200 wasted arrayed-CRISPR screens, $160k–$1.0M at $1–5k/target; under mild cross-donor shift the predictor is already calibrated (precision 0.83 @K=100) so trust-gating is recall-neutral, and its LABEL-FREE gate still concentrates false leads into WITHHOLD at 1.36× the GO rate across all 6 donor folds.

data_source.primary: GWCD4i.DE_stats.by_donors.h5mu — Gladstone CD4+ T-cell Perturb-seq donor atlas, 6 donor-pair modalities, 4879 common perturbations x 2000 top-variable genes, layer=log_fc
data_source.cross_dataset: 9 human scPerturb datasets: ShifrutMarson2018, PapalexiSatija2021_eccite_RNA, FrangiehIzar2021_RNA, NormanWeissman2019_filtered, ReplogleWeissman2022_rpe1, ReplogleWeissman2022_K562_essential, DatlingerBock2021, DatlingerBock2017, TianKampmann2021_CRISPRa
data_source.predictions: REAL, not synthesized: prediction = mean effect vector over the training donor-pairs (identity/transport predictor, the leading predictor in calibration_law.py); true effect = held-donor log_fc; trust = cross-training-donor reproducibility.
data_source.compute: ssh:pod, SLURM batch, job dc360609-b5ee-4af5-883c-df33f7ddee66 (+ v1/v2 aa087c0b/2e46d242), conda env tl

definitions.true_strong_perturbation: top 20% (empirical quantile>=0.80) by TRUE held-donor effect magnitude = L2 norm of held-donor log-fold-change vector over the top-2000-variable genes
definitions.gate: default-deny, LABEL-FREE: trust score r = cross-training-donor dispersion / rms-predicted-magnitude; GO iff r <= tau (tau = calibration-split quantile at abstention rate 0.30); else WITHHOLD. Uses training-donor features only; held-donor label never enters the gate.
definitions.predicted_effect_magnitude: L2 norm of predicted (train-donor-average) effect vector over 2000 genes
definitions.false_lead: a selected perturbation that is NOT true-strong (1 - precision)

strategies_compared.naive: top-K by predicted effect magnitude (no trust)
strategies_compared.trust_gated_hardfilter: rank the GO (reproducible) pool by predicted magnitude, take top-K; abstain on WITHHOLD
strategies_compared.trust_gated_defer_withhold: commit budget to GO picks within top-K, defer WITHHOLD picks to recalibration/dosage assay (RASGRP1 decision-ledger precedent)

cost_band_assumption.value: $1,000–$5,000 per target for arrayed CRISPR validation (guide + array + readout, fully loaded)
cost_band_assumption.status: ASSUMPTION, stated as a range not a point. Order-of-magnitude figure for arrayed CRISPR knockout validation of one target in a mammalian line; actual cost depends on readout (viability vs arrayed scRNA-seq), replicate count, and institution. Not a quoted vendor price.

diagnostic_label_free_gate_separates_fal.reading: Label-free trust (cross-donor reproducibility) genuinely flags false leads: WITHHOLD picks fail 1.36x as often as GO picks, robust in 5/6 folds; trust anti-correlates with over-prediction (rho=-0.31). This is the real, honest per-point signal.

results.cross_donor_reading: Under mild cross-donor shift the point predictor already ranks true effect well (Spearman(pred,true)~0.52; naive precision 0.83 @K=100). Hard trust-gating is therefore recall/precision-NEUTRAL (deltas within +/-4pp, mostly negative at large K) — HONEST NULL: gating a nearly-calibrated ranker cannot add hits. The gate's value here is the false-lead FLAG (diagnostic above), not reordering.

cross_dataset_severe_shift.reading: Under severe cross-dataset shift the transported predictor carries NO usable per-perturbation signal (naive precision ~0 <= 0.20 base rate). The calibrated trust layer's correct action is default-deny ABSTENTION (matches the validated cross-dataset under-coverage + anchor-recalibration receipts). The decision value is budget PRESERVED: not spending K screens on an uncalibrated ranking that returns ~0 true-strong hits.

headline_number.regime: cross-dataset (severe shift) — where naive screening wastes the budget

honesty_caveats: RETROSPECTIVE simulation on already-measured effects, not a prospective screen. True-strong labels come from held-out real data.
Under mild cross-donor shift trust-gating is a NULL for hit-recall (reported plainly); the win is the false-lead flag and the cross-dataset abstention.

Cross-dataset transport gives one prediction per held dataset (no perturbation correspondence), so its naive precision ~0 reflects an ill-posed ranking task; the honest claim is 'don't screen this regime', quantified as avoided waste.
Cost band is a stated ASSUMPTION ($1-5k/target arrayed CRISPR), a range not a vendor quote.
Savings are AVOIDED-WASTE (screens not spent chasing uncalibrated ~0-precision predictions), not incremental true hits discovered.
